---
title: "nitrix.semiring.matmul"
generated: true
signature-hash: 94d17a976965e107
description: "Public [`semiring_matmul`](nitrix-semiring-matmul/semiring_matmul.qmd) with three-level backend selection."
listing:
  id: nitrix-semiring-matmul-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-semiring-matmul/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.semiring.matmul`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Public [`semiring_matmul`](nitrix-semiring-matmul/semiring_matmul.qmd) with three-level backend selection.

The public entry point accepts a left operand of shape `(..., m, k)`
and a right operand of shape `(..., k, n)`, an optional `semiring`
algebra, and a `backend` selector, returning the semiring-generalised
product of shape `(..., m, n)`.

Leading batch dims are broadcast-compatible.  The 2-D core is dispatched
to either [`reference_semiring_matmul`](nitrix-semiring-matmul/reference_semiring_matmul.qmd) (pure JAX) or the Pallas /
Triton kernel `semiring_matmul_pallas`.  If the Pallas path cannot
tile a given shape and algebra combination, the call falls back to JAX
and emits exactly one `NitrixBackendFallback` warning per
`(function, shape, dtype, backend)` per process; see
`nitrix._internal.backend` for the environment-variable knobs.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-semiring-matmul-symbols}
:::
